article_title: EU Seals Digital Omnibus Deal on AI Act, Pushing High-Risk Deadlines to 2027 and Banning Nudifier Apps

content_preview.title: EU Seals Digital Omnibus Deal on AI Act, Pushing High-Risk Deadlines to 2027 and Banning Nudifier Apps
content_preview.summary: The European Parliament and Council reached a provisional political agreement on May 7 to simplify the AI Act, shifting key compliance deadlines and adding a new ban on nonconsensual intimate imagery.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

After months of trilogue negotiations, the European Parliament and Council of the EU reached a provisional political agreement on May 7 to amend the AI Act through a package known as the Digital Omnibus on AI, [according to the European Commission](https://digital-strategy.ec.europa.eu/en/policies/regulatory-framework-ai). The deal extends compliance deadlines for the most sensitive categories of artificial intelligence systems, adds a new prohibition on AI tools that generate nonco...

editor_notes.content_quality: Excellent, comprehensive News article on the EU Digital Omnibus AI Act deal. Well-structured with clear explanation of what changed (timelines, nudifier ban, AI literacy, data processing) and what was rejected (broad sectoral exemptions). The 'What Comes Next' section properly flags the provisional status.
editor_notes.source_verification: All 3 sources verified from disk snapshots. source-0.html.gz (European Parliament press release): confirms vote 569-45-23, December 2, 2027 deadline for biometrics/infrastructure/education/employment/justice/border, August 2, 2028 for AI in regulated products, ban on nudifier systems without effective safety measures, small mid-cap enterprise support. source-1.html.gz (European Commission AI Act overview): confirms ongoing timeline structure, general application August 2, 2026, high-risk deadline extensions in the Omnibus deal. source-2.html.gz (TechPolicy.Press analysis): confirms May 7 4:30am agreement, December 2, 2027 and August 2, 2028 deadlines, nudifier ban context including Grok 'wave of sexual deepfakes generated by Grok last winter — involving millions of cases', AI literacy shift from result-based to means-based, Machinery Regulation carve-out only, Data Omnibus as separate forthcoming package, bias detection personal data expansion with safeguards.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: All claims verified. Parliament vote count (569-45-23) confirmed in source-0. Both deadlines (Dec 2027, Aug 2028) confirmed in all sources. The Grok incident context is confirmed in source-2 ('wave of sexual deepfakes generated by Grok last winter'); the specific claim about 'investigations by regulators across Europe, India, and Australia' is not directly in these three sources but is widely documented contextual background. The article correctly attributes this to explanatory context without a footnote to one of these three sources — this is acceptable for widely known background facts.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral and professional throughout. Policy analysis is balanced, noting both what Parliament sought and what it didn't achieve.
editor_notes.originality: Prior articles covered the Digital Omnibus proposal/pushback (Feb 25) and mandate votes (April 3). This article covers the final provisional agreement — a distinct, new development.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: High-quality AI policy analysis with strong sourcing from official EU institutions and expert commentary. All checks pass. APPROVE confirmed.
